ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Most Merciful
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Most Compassionate

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Taught the Qur'an,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: taught the Quran,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Created man,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: created humanity,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And] taught him eloquence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹and˺ taught them speech.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The sun and the moon [move] by precise calculation,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The sun and the moon ˹travel˺ with precision.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the stars and trees prostrate.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The stars and the trees bow down ˹in submission˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the heaven He raised and imposed the balance
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for the sky, He raised it ˹high˺, and set the balance ˹of justice˺

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That you not transgress within the balance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that you do not defraud the scales.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَقِيمُواْ ٱلۡوَزۡنَ بِٱلۡقِسۡطِ وَلَا تُخۡسِرُواْ ٱلۡمِيزَانَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waaqeemoo alwazna bialqisti wala tukhsiroo almeezana
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And establish weight in justice and do not make deficient the balance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Weigh with justice, and do not give short measure.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the earth He laid [out] for the creatures.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He laid out the earth for all beings.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Therein is fruit and palm trees having sheaths [of dates]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In it are fruit, palm trees with date stalks,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And grain having husks and scented plants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and grain with husks, and aromatic plants.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you ˹humans and jinn˺ both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He created man from clay like [that of] pottery.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He created humankind from ˹sounding˺ clay like pottery,

ayahs.textArabic: وَخَلَقَ ٱلۡجَآنَّ مِن مَّارِجٖ مِّن نَّارٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakhalaqa aljanna min marijin min narin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and created jinn from a ˹smokeless˺ flame of fire.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [He is] Lord of the two sunrises and Lord of the two sunsets.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is˺ Lord of the two easts and the two wests.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He released the two seas, meeting [side by side];
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He merges the two bodies of ˹fresh and salt˺ water,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Between them is a barrier [so] neither of them transgresses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: yet between them is a barrier they never cross.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From both of them emerge pearl and coral.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Out of both ˹waters˺ come forth pearls and coral.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَهُ ٱلۡجَوَارِ ٱلۡمُنشَـَٔاتُ فِي ٱلۡبَحۡرِ كَٱلۡأَعۡلَٰمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walahu aljawari almunshaatu fee albahri kaalaAAlami
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to Him belong the ships [with sails] elevated in the sea like mountains.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: To Him belong the ships with raised sails, sailing through the seas like mountains.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Everyone upon the earth will perish,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Every being on earth is bound to perish.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَبۡقَىٰ وَجۡهُ رَبِّكَ ذُو ٱلۡجَلَٰلِ وَٱلۡإِكۡرَامِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayabqa wajhu rabbika thoo aljalali waalikrami
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there will remain the Face of your Lord, Owner of Majesty and Honor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Only your Lord Himself, full of Majesty and Honour, will remain ˹forever˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: يَسۡـَٔلُهُۥ مَن فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ كُلَّ يَوۡمٍ هُوَ فِي شَأۡنٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yasaluhu man fee alssamawati waalardi kulla yawmin huwa fee shanin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Whoever is within the heavens and earth asks Him; every day He is bringing about a matter.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: All those in the heavens and the earth are dependent on Him. Day in and day out He has something to bring about.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We will attend to you, O prominent beings.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We will soon attend to you ˹for judgment˺, O two multitudes ˹of jinn and humans˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰمَعۡشَرَ ٱلۡجِنِّ وَٱلۡإِنسِ إِنِ ٱسۡتَطَعۡتُمۡ أَن تَنفُذُواْ مِنۡ أَقۡطَارِ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱنفُذُواْۚ لَا تَنفُذُونَ إِلَّا بِسُلۡطَٰنٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya maAAshara aljinni waalinsi ini istataAAtum an tanfuthoo min aqtari alssamawati waalardi faonfuthoo la tanfuthoona illa bisultanin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O company of jinn and mankind, if you are able to pass beyond the regions of the heavens and the earth, then pass. You will not pass except by authority [from Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O assembly of jinn and humans! If you can penetrate beyond the realms of the heavens and the earth, then do so. ˹But˺ you cannot do that without ˹Our˺ authority.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: يُرۡسَلُ عَلَيۡكُمَا شُوَاظٞ مِّن نَّارٖ وَنُحَاسٞ فَلَا تَنتَصِرَانِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yursalu AAalaykuma shuwathun min narin wanuhasun fala tantasirani
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There will be sent upon you a flame of fire and smoke, and you will not defend yourselves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Flames of fire and ˹molten˺ copper will be sent against you, and you will not be able to defend one another.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا ٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَكَانَتۡ وَرۡدَةٗ كَٱلدِّهَانِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha inshaqqati alssamao fakanat wardatan kaalddihani
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the heaven is split open and becomes rose-colored like oil -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹How horrible will it be˺ when the heavens will split apart, becoming rose-red like ˹burnt˺ oil!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: فَيَوۡمَئِذٖ لَّا يُسۡـَٔلُ عَن ذَنۢبِهِۦٓ إِنسٞ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fayawmaithin la yusalu AAan thanbihi insun wala jannun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then on that Day none will be asked about his sin among men or jinn.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day there will be no need for any human or jinn to be asked about their sins.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: يُعۡرَفُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ بِسِيمَٰهُمۡ فَيُؤۡخَذُ بِٱلنَّوَٰصِي وَٱلۡأَقۡدَامِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: YuAArafu almujrimoona biseemahum fayukhathu bialnnawasee waalaqdami
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The criminals will be known by their marks, and they will be seized by the forelocks and the feet.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The wicked will be recognized by their appearance, then will be seized by ˹their˺ forelocks and feet.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِي يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hathihi jahannamu allatee yukaththibu biha almujrimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This is Hell, which the criminals deny.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Hell which the wicked denied.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will go around between it and scalding water, heated [to the utmost degree].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will alternate between its flames and scalding water.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But for he who has feared the position of his Lord are two gardens -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And whoever is in awe of standing before their Lord will have two Gardens.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Having [spreading] branches.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Both will be˺ with lush branches.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In both of them are two springs, flowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In each ˹Garden˺ will be two flowing springs.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In both of them are of every fruit, two kinds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In each will be two types of every fruit.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ فُرُشِۭ بَطَآئِنُهَا مِنۡ إِسۡتَبۡرَقٖۚ وَجَنَى ٱلۡجَنَّتَيۡنِ دَانٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Muttakieena AAala furushin batainuha min istabraqin wajana aljannatayni danin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They are] reclining on beds whose linings are of silk brocade, and the fruit of the two gardens is hanging low.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those ˹believers˺ will recline on furnishings lined with rich brocade. And the fruit of both Gardens will hang within reach.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: فِيهِنَّ قَٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرۡفِ لَمۡ يَطۡمِثۡهُنَّ إِنسٞ قَبۡلَهُمۡ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Feehinna qasiratu alttarfi lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In them are women limiting [their] glances, untouched before them by man or jinni -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In both ˹Gardens˺ will be maidens of modest gaze, who no human or jinn has ever touched before.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As if they were rubies and coral.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those ˹maidens˺ will be ˹as elegant˺ as rubies and coral.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is the reward for good [anything] but good?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is there any reward for goodness except goodness?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And below them both [in excellence] are two [other] gardens -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And below these two ˹Gardens˺ will be two others.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Dark green [in color].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Both will be dark green.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In both of them are two springs, spouting.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In each will be two gushing springs.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In both of them are fruit and palm trees and pomegranates.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In both will be fruit, palm trees, and pomegranates.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In them are good and beautiful women -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In all Gardens will be noble, pleasant mates.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Fair ones reserved in pavilions -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be˺ maidens with gorgeous eyes, reserved in pavilions.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: لَمۡ يَطۡمِثۡهُنَّ إِنسٞ قَبۡلَهُمۡ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Untouched before them by man or jinni -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: No human or jinn has ever touched these ˹maidens˺ before.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ رَفۡرَفٍ خُضۡرٖ وَعَبۡقَرِيٍّ حِسَانٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Muttakieena AAala rafrafin khudrin waAAabqariyyin hisanin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Reclining on green cushions and beautiful fine carpets.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: All ˹believers˺ will be reclining on green cushions and splendid carpets.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then which of your Lord’s favours will you both deny?

ayahs.textArabic: تَبَٰرَكَ ٱسۡمُ رَبِّكَ ذِي ٱلۡجَلَٰلِ وَٱلۡإِكۡرَامِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tabaraka ismu rabbika thee aljalali waalikrami
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blessed is the name of your Lord, Owner of Majesty and Honor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Blessed is the Name of your Lord, full of Majesty and Honour.
